trait.name: Glass-Cannon Minion
entries: This creature's attacks are calibrated to Challenge 17 (tier 4), but its Hit Points are deliberately minion-grade—it hits like an endgame threat and dies like a mook. Its listed CR reflects the danger of its attacks, not its durability: a single solid hit or area spell from a well-equipped 17th-level character usually drops it. For encounter budgeting, treat it as far less durable than a true CR 17 monster (see the Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer for roster math).

trait.name: Paradox-Touched
entries: The goblin was recopied by the paradox around Vorthrax: its hide is tinted violet and its eyes shed faint starlight (Dim Light in a 5-foot radius). It has Advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}—it has already seen the end of the world.

trait.name: Nimble Escape
entries: The goblin takes the {@action Disengage} or {@action Hide} action as a Bonus Action on each of its turns.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The goblin makes two Paradox Scimitar attacks.

action.name: Paradox Scimitar
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 13}, reach 5 ft. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Slashing damage plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Necrotic damage.

reaction.name: Unmade Flicker
entries: When the goblin is reduced to 0 Hit Points, its body tears into a thumbnail of night sky and is gone. Each creature within 5 feet of it takes 7 ({@damage 3d4}) Necrotic damage from the imploding seam.

A frontline cutter of the Recopied Warband—a violet shadow of a goblin that swings a blade edged in unmade dark. Part of the Recopied Warband; see the {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer|ParadoxWarband} for the full encounter.

trait.name: Glass-Cannon Minion
entries: This creature's attacks are calibrated to Challenge 17 (tier 4), but its Hit Points are deliberately minion-grade—it hits like an endgame threat and dies like a mook. Its listed CR reflects the danger of its attacks, not its durability: a single solid hit or area spell from a well-equipped 17th-level character usually drops it. For encounter budgeting, treat it as far less durable than a true CR 17 monster (see the Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer for roster math).

trait.name: Paradox-Touched
entries: The goblin was recopied by the paradox around Vorthrax: its hide is tinted violet and its eyes shed faint starlight (Dim Light in a 5-foot radius). It has Advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}—it has already seen the end of the world.

trait.name: Nimble Escape
entries: The goblin takes the {@action Disengage} or {@action Hide} action as a Bonus Action on each of its turns.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The goblin makes two Paradox Shortbow attacks.

action.name: Paradox Shortbow
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 13}, range 80/320 ft. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Piercing damage plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Force damage.

action.name: Starfall Volley {@recharge 5}
entries: The goblin looses an arrow that bursts into falling starlight at a point it can see within 150 feet. {@actSave dex} {@dc 20}, each creature in a 15-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on that point. {@actSaveFail} 36 ({@damage 8d8}) Force damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

reaction.name: Unmade Flicker
entries: When the goblin is reduced to 0 Hit Points, its body tears into a thumbnail of night sky and is gone. Each creature within 5 feet of it takes 7 ({@damage 3d4}) Force damage from the imploding seam.

A recopied skirmisher that fires arrows of bottled starlight from the edges of the fight. Part of the Recopied Warband; see the {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer|ParadoxWarband} for the full encounter.

trait.name: Glass-Cannon Minion
entries: This creature's attacks and spells are calibrated to Challenge 17 (tier 4), but its Hit Points are deliberately minion-grade—it hits like an endgame threat and dies like a mook. Its listed CR reflects the danger of its magic, not its durability: a single solid hit or area spell from a well-equipped 17th-level character usually drops it.

trait.name: Paradox-Touched
entries: The goblin was recopied by the paradox around Vorthrax: its hide is tinted violet and its eyes shed faint starlight (Dim Light in a 5-foot radius). It has Advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}—it has already seen the end of the world.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The goblin casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 20}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +12 to hit with spell attacks}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The goblin casts a spell or makes two Hex Bolt attacks.

action.name: Hex Bolt
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 12}, range 120 ft. {@h}27 ({@damage 5d8 + 4}) Necrotic damage, and the target can't regain Hit Points until the end of the goblin's next turn.

reaction.name: Curse of the Unmade
entries: When a creature the goblin can see within 60 feet reduces it to 0 Hit Points, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 20} Wisdom saving throw or take 22 ({@damage 5d8}) Psychic damage and have Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the end of its next turn, as a stranger's memories flood in.

entries.name: The Recopied Warband
entries: This warband of goblins and kobolds wandered too long inside the violet ring around Vorthrax's sleeping-hollow, and the paradox recopied them. Each is the same creature it always was—just repainted for the end of the world: hide gone violet, eyes lit with stray starlight, every blow edged with a sliver of the unmade dark. They are louder, faster, and far deadlier than any goblin or kobold has a right to be... and just as fragile as they ever were. The hexer leads, because it remembers leading—over and over, in lives that no longer happened.

entries.name: Design: Glass-Cannon Minions (CR 17)
entries: Every member of the warband is built as a {@b glass cannon}: its attacks, save DCs, and damage are calibrated to Challenge 17 (tier 4), so a single one is a genuine threat to a well-equipped 17th-level character—but its Hit Points are minion-grade (45–66), so it drops to almost any focused hit or area spell. The CR 17 label is the threat of the attack, not the toughness of the body. They have just enough Hit Points to survive the alpha strike of a tier-4 party and take one real turn—which is exactly when they bite.

This breaks the normal CR-to-XP math on purpose. By the book, six CR 17 monsters would be an absurd 108,000 XP; in practice these die so fast that the effective difficulty is roughly that of treating each as a {@b CR 6–7} creature. Budget by their fragility, not their label.

The six variants: {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Cutter|ParadoxWarband} and {@creature Paradox-Touched Kobold Clawfoot|ParadoxWarband} (melee warriors), {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Stinger|ParadoxWarband} and {@creature Paradox-Touched Kobold Slinger|ParadoxWarband} (archers), and the {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer|ParadoxWarband} and {@creature Paradox-Touched Kobold Star-Scale|ParadoxWarband} (casters).

entries.name: Running It for Four Well-Equipped 17th-Level Characters
entries: This is the same party that fights Vorthrax: four 17th-level characters, each with a legendary item and a very rare item. The XP budgets for them are Low 18,000 / Moderate 28,800 / High 46,800. Because these minions are glass cannons, the fight is {@b swingy}—whoever lands the first big turn shapes it. Suggested rosters:

items: {@b Sharp brawl (recommended):} 1 caster + 1 archer + 2 warriors (4 total)—a tense, fast 2–3 round fight that ends when the party's area damage lands.

{@b The full warband:} both casters + 2 archers + 4 warriors (8 total)—genuinely dangerous in round 1 against a tier-4 party, then collapses quickly. Open with the casters' control and the archers' Volley/Scatter before the party scatters them.

{@b Endless tide:} feed them in waves of 4–6 each round to evoke the region recopying its dead—thematically perfect, and it lets you dial lethality live. This is the recommended way to threaten a party this strong: attrition, not a single alpha.

entries: Tactics: casters open with {@spell hunger of hadar|XPHB}, {@spell synaptic static|XPHB}, or {@spell hold monster|XPHB} to pin the party; archers spend {@b Starfall Volley}/{@b Scatter Shot} on clusters; warriors use Nimble Escape and Pack Tactics to gang a single isolated character. Lean into the {@b Unmade Flicker} death bursts—every minion that dies stings whoever killed it, so melee characters take a slow toll wading through the horde.

Flavor note: these are the literal punchline of the campaign's running joke—the level-20 goblin in a different color. When the party recognizes the silhouette of a common goblin and then it hits like a CR 17, that dissonance is the point.

trait.name: Glass-Cannon Minion
entries: This creature's attacks are calibrated to Challenge 17 (tier 4), but its Hit Points are deliberately minion-grade—it hits like an endgame threat and dies like a mook. Its listed CR reflects the danger of its attacks, not its durability: a single solid hit or area spell from a well-equipped 17th-level character usually drops it. For encounter budgeting, treat it as far less durable than a true CR 17 monster (see the Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer for roster math).

trait.name: Paradox-Touched
entries: The kobold was recopied by the paradox around Vorthrax: its scales are tinted violet and its eyes shed faint starlight (Dim Light in a 5-foot radius). It has Advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}, and the recopying has burned away its kind's usual Sunlight Sensitivity.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The kobold has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the kobold's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated} condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The kobold makes two Paradox Claw attacks.

action.name: Paradox Claw
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 13}, reach 5 ft. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) Slashing damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Necrotic damage.

bonus.name: Skitter
entries: The kobold takes the {@action Disengage} action.

reaction.name: Unmade Flicker
entries: When the kobold is reduced to 0 Hit Points, its body tears into a thumbnail of night sky and is gone. Each creature within 5 feet of it takes 7 ({@damage 3d4}) Necrotic damage from the imploding seam.
A recopied kobold that fights in violet packs, claws trailing unmade dark. Part of the Recopied Warband; see the {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer|ParadoxWarband} for the full encounter.

entries: This creature's attacks are calibrated to Challenge 17 (tier 4), but its Hit Points are deliberately minion-grade—it hits like an endgame threat and dies like a mook. entries: The kobold was recopied by the paradox around Vorthrax: its scales are tinted violet and its eyes shed faint starlight (Dim Light in a 5-foot radius). trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The kobold has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the kobold's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated} condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The kobold makes two Paradox Sling attacks.

action.name: Paradox Sling
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 13}, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Bludgeoning damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Force damage.

action.name: Scatter Shot {@recharge 5}
entries: The kobold whirls a spray of starlit shot in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSave dex} {@dc 19}, each creature in that area. {@actSaveFail} 28 ({@damage 8d6}) Force damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

reaction.name: Unmade Flicker
entries: When the kobold is reduced to 0 Hit Points, its body tears into a thumbnail of night sky and is gone. Each creature within 5 feet of it takes 7 ({@damage 3d4}) Force damage from the imploding seam.

A recopied kobold that pelts the party with bottled starlight from behind its packmates. Part of the Recopied Warband; see the {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer|ParadoxWarband} for the full encounter.

entries: This creature's spells are calibrated to Challenge 17 (tier 4), but its Hit Points are deliberately minion-grade—it hits like an endgame threat and dies like a mook. It has Advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}, and the recopying has burned away its kind's usual Sunlight Sensitivity.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The kobold has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the kobold's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated} condition.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The kobold casts one of the following spells, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 20}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +12 to hit with spell attacks}):

1e: {@spell fireball|XPHB} (cast as "Starburst" at 5th level; 35 (10d6) Force damage, save {@dc 20})

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The kobold makes two Star Bolt attacks.

action.name: Star Bolt
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 12}, range 120 ft. {@h}22 ({@damage 5d8}) Force damage.

reaction.name: Unmade Flicker
entries: When the kobold is reduced to 0 Hit Points, its body tears into a thumbnail of night sky and is gone. Each creature within 5 feet of it takes 7 ({@damage 3d4}) Force damage from the imploding seam.

A recopied scale-sorcerer whose bloodline magic now burns in the colors of the unmade sky—its 1/day {@b Starburst} (a Force-damage {@spell fireball|XPHB}) is the warband's deadliest single moment. Part of the Recopied Warband; see the {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer|ParadoxWarband} for the full encounter.
